In 1851, Queen Victoria fell in love with this pattern.

scenes.overlay_text: Bring Royal Elegance to your home. ✨ Follow for more.

caption:
Did you know Queen Victoria once stopped in her tracks just to admire a teacup? 🦋✨

At the 1851 Great Exhibition in London, a masterpiece from a small Hungarian village stole the spotlight. The Queen was so mesmerized by the delicate butterflies and peonies that she ordered a full set for Windsor Castle.

This is the birth of the legendary 'Victoria Pattern' by Herend.

Beyond luxury, it's a testament to timeless craftsmanship. To this day, every piece is hand-painted by artisans who carry 200 years of tradition in their fingertips. No machines, just pure art.

True luxury isn't just about the price tag; it's about the heritage we pass down. If you could own one royal piece, what would it be? ☕️🌸
